name: Web Application
description: Host a website or web API that scales automatically with your traffic. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: appName
fields.label: What should we call this application?
fields.helpText: This will be part of your application's web address.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it run?
fields.helpText: Choose a location close to your users.

fields.name: planSize
fields.label: How much power does it need?
fields.helpText: Start small — you can scale up later.

name: Virtual Machine
description: A virtual computer in the cloud for running software, tools, or development environments.

fields.name: vmName
fields.label: What should we call this virtual machine?
fields.helpText: Use a descriptive name for what this machine will do.

fields.name: osType
fields.label: Which operating system?
fields.helpText: Choose the OS you're most comfortable with.

fields.name: vmSize
fields.label: How powerful should it be?
fields.helpText: Larger sizes cost more but handle heavier workloads.

name: Managed Database
description: A fully managed database — no maintenance needed. Store and query structured data for your applications. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: dbName
fields.label: What should we call this database?
fields.helpText: Use a name that describes what data it will store.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it run?
fields.helpText: For best performance, choose the same location as your application.

fields.name: storageGB
fields.label: How much storage (in GB)?
fields.helpText: Start with 32 GB — you can increase this later.

fields.name: engineVersion
fields.label: Which database engine version?
fields.helpText: PostgreSQL is the most versatile choice for most projects.

name: File Storage
description: Store files, documents, images, and data securely — accessible from anywhere in the world. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: storageName
fields.label: What should we call this storage?
fields.helpText: Lowercase letters and numbers only, no spaces.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should files be stored?
fields.helpText: Choose a location close to where you'll be accessing the files.

fields.name: accessTier
fields.label: How often will you access these files?
fields.helpText: Hot is faster but costs more for infrequent access.

fields.name: redundancy
fields.label: How many copies should we keep?
fields.helpText: More copies means better protection against data loss.

options.label: 3 copies in the same location — Basic protection
6 copies across distant locations — Best protection

name: Private Network
description: An isolated private network so your cloud resources can communicate securely without going through the internet. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: vnetName
fields.label: What should we call this network?
fields.helpText: Use a name that describes what this network connects.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it be created?
fields.helpText: Must be in the same location as the resources using this network.

fields.name: addressSpace
fields.label: What IP address range should this network use?
fields.helpText: The default works for most setups. Leave it as-is if you're unsure.

fields.name: subnetName
fields.label: What should the first subnet be called?
fields.helpText: Subnets help you organize and isolate groups of resources.

fields.name: subnetRange
fields.label: What IP range should this subnet use?
fields.helpText: Must be within your network's address range. The default works for most cases.

name: Secret Manager
description: A secure vault for storing passwords, API keys, certificates, and encryption keys — separate from your code. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: vaultName
fields.label: What should we call this secret manager?
fields.helpText: Must be globally unique. Try your-project-secrets.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it be created?
fields.helpText: For best performance, choose the same location as your application.

fields.name: softDelete
fields.label: Enable recovery period for deleted secrets?
fields.helpText: Recommended. Lets you recover accidentally deleted secrets within 90 days.

fields.name: purgeProtection
fields.label: Prevent permanent deletion?
fields.helpText: Prevents anyone from permanently deleting secrets until the recovery period ends.

fields.name: kvSku
fields.label: Which Key Vault tier?
fields.helpText: Premium supports HSM-backed keys.

fields.name: accessModel
fields.label: How should applications access secrets?
fields.helpText: Role-based access is the modern and recommended approach.

name: Container Application
description: Run a containerized application without managing any servers — just provide your Docker image. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: appName
fields.label: What should we call this application?
fields.helpText: This will also be part of your app's URL.

fields.name: containerImage
fields.label: What container image should we run?
fields.helpText: The Docker image name, e.g. myregistry/myapp:v1.0

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it run?
fields.helpText: Choose a location close to your users.

fields.name: minReplicas
fields.label: Minimum number of running copies?
fields.helpText: Set to 0 to scale down to zero when not in use (saves cost, but adds startup delay).

fields.name: maxReplicas
fields.label: Maximum number of copies during high traffic?
fields.helpText: The app will automatically create more copies when traffic increases, up to this limit.

fields.name: externalAccess
fields.label: Allow access from the internet?
fields.helpText: Enable if your app needs to be reachable from outside your organization's network.

name: Automation Workflow
description: A visual workflow that automates processes by connecting services. Triggered by an HTTP call — no code required. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it run?
fields.helpText: Choose a location close to the services it will connect.

name: Message Queue
description: A reliable message broker that decouples services — send messages from one system and process them in another at any pace. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it run?
fields.helpText: Choose a location close to the services sending and receiving messages.

fields.name: tier
fields.label: Which tier do you need?
fields.helpText: Standard adds topics and subscriptions for fan-out messaging.

name: Event Broadcaster
description: Publish events from your application and let multiple subscribers react to them independently in real time. Also deploys a Log Analytics workspace and Key Vault for observability and secrets governance.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Where should it run?
fields.helpText: Choose a location close to the services publishing events.

fields.name: topicName
fields.label: What should we call this event hub?
fields.helpText: Use a name that describes the events it will carry.

fields.name: inputSchema
fields.label: Which event schema should publishers use?
fields.helpText: CloudEvents is the open standard — prefer it for new systems.
